lede: A look at June 3 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Sutee Mcateer, Keilor and Rafael Nadal, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Academy of the Distrustful
events.desc: Reconstitution of the Academy of the Distrustful in the Sala Dalmases of the Historical Archive of the City of Barcelona in Barcelona.

events.title: Tiananmen Square
events.desc: The government of China sends troops to force protesters out of Tiananmen Square after seven weeks of occupation.

events.title: Shlomo Argov
events.desc: The Israeli ambassador to the United Kingdom, Shlomo Argov, is shot on a London street; he survives but is left paralysed.

events.title: Maurice Herzog
events.desc: Herzog and Lachenal of the French Annapurna expedition become the first climbers to reach the summit of an 8,000-metre peak.

events.title: Wehrmacht
events.desc: World War II: The Wehrmacht razes the Greek village of Kandanos to the ground and murders 180 of its inhabitants.

events.title: Franz Rademacher
events.desc: Franz Rademacher proposes plans to make Madagascar the "Jewish homeland", an idea that had first been considered by 19th century journalist Theodor Herzl.

events.title: Liverpool F.C.
events.desc: Liverpool F.C. is founded by John Houlding.

events.title: Great auk
events.desc: The last pair of great auks is killed.

events.title: Jack Jouett
events.desc: Jack Jouett begins his midnight ride to warn Thomas Jefferson and the Virginia legislature of an impending British raid.

events.title: Pope Alexander VII
events.desc: Pope Alexander VII appoints François de Laval vicar apostolic in New France.

births.name: Dean Pay
births.line: Australian rugby league player and coach

births.name: Torsten Wiesel
births.line: Swedish neurophysiologist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate

deaths.text: Brian Talboys, New Zealand journalist and politician, 7th Deputy Prime Minister of New Zealand
Puig Aubert, German-French rugby player and coach
Michael Gaughan (Irish republican), Irish Republican died on hunger strike

observances.text: Christian feast day: Charles Lwanga and Companions (Roman Catholic Church), and its related observances: Martyrs' Day (Uganda)
